sections.paragraph:
to languish, to droop, prop. to hang down the head. Kindred is אָבַל which see. In Kal part. pass. of a drooping heart, Eze 16:30.

Pulal אֻמְלַל [“only in poetry”].-

(1) to languish, prop. used of plants hanging down their heads, Isa 24:7 hence used of fields, of a sick person, Psa 6:3, where אֻמְלַל is for מְאֻמְלָל [“so Maurer”].

(2) to be sad, Isa 19:8 of a land laid waste, Isa 24:4 33:9 of walls thrown down, Lam 2:8. It is only found in poetic language. But in prose there is-
